perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Invoices to NIST. The Project Manager shall prepare and submit monthly ---------------- invoices to NIST which shall include appropriate cost breakdowns as required. Such invoices will be submitted by the Project Manager by the last day of the following month for charges incurred in a particular month, and will include charges of each Participant and all subcontractors, as appropriate. It is anticipated that NIST will issue payment by check directly
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Invoices to NIST. The Project Manager shall prepare and submit monthly ---------------- invoices to NIST which shall include appropriate cost breakdowns as required. Such invoices will be submitted by the Project Manager by the last day of the following month for charges incurred in a particular month, except if 75% of the participants submits there invoices by the 15th. In that case, the project manager has the right to submit the invoices immediately . It is anticipated that NIST will issue payment by check directly
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by introducing a conditional clause to the deadline for submitting invoices to NIST. The original text specifies that invoices will be submitted by the Project Manager by the last day of the following month. However, the modified text adds a clause that allows the Project Manager to submit invoices immediately if 75% of the participants submit their invoices by the 15th. This contradicts the original deadline, creating ambiguity and uncertainty as to when invoices will be submitted to NIST.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Invoices to NIST. The Project Manager shall prepare and submit monthly ---------------- invoices to NIST which shall include appropriate cost breakdowns as required. Such invoices will be submitted by the Project Manager by the last day of the following month for charges incurred in a particular month, except if 75% of the participants submits there invoices by the 15th. In that case, the project manager has the right to submit the invoices immediately . It is anticipated that NIST will issue payment by check directly
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a conditional clause to the invoice submission deadline, stating that the Project Manager can submit invoices immediately if 75% of participants submit their invoices by the 15th. This directly contradicts the original statement in the same section, which specifies that invoices will be submitted by the last day of the following month. This meets the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract (in this case, the same section after modification) provide conflicting deadlines, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Prototype Even Period. ---------------------- A. Options of GM and GE. Upon completion or other termination of the -------------------- Project, the parties agree that GE and/or GM shall have an option, exercisable upon written notice to SMS received within sixty [60] days after such completion or other termination, to have SMS maintain the "bread board" apparatus or the "demonstrator" apparatus from the Project and make such apparatus available to GE and/or GM for testing and/or evaluation purposes during a period of time ("Prototype Evaluation Period"). The Probe Evaluation Period shall begin upon receipt of such notice and end upon the earliest of: (1) the second anniversary of such completion or other termination, or (2) the delivery of at least one commercial Imaging System to each of GE and GM, or (3) the delivery of one commercial Imaging System to either GE or GM, where the other party fails to fulfill the requirements of paragraph 6.3 B2 below; or (4) SMS' delivery of the twelfth commercial Imaging System.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Prototype Even Period. ---------------------- A. Options of GM and GE. Upon completion or other termination of the -------------------- Project, the parties agree that GE and/or GM shall have an option, exercisable upon written notice to SMS received within sixty [60] days after such completion or other termination, unless SMS offers a competing technology, in which case the option period is reduced to thirty [30] days, to have SMS maintain the "bread board" apparatus or the "demonstrator" apparatus from the Project and make such apparatus available to GE and/or GM for testing and/or evaluation purposes during a period of time ("Prototype Evaluation Period"). The Probe Evaluation Period shall begin upon receipt of such notice and end upon the earliest of: (1) the second anniversary of such completion or other termination, or (2) the delivery of at least one commercial Imaging System to each of GE and GM, or (3) the delivery of one commercial Imaging System to either GE or GM, where the other party fails to fulfill the requirements of paragraph 6.3 B2 below; or (4) SMS' delivery of the twelfth commercial Imaging System.
perturbation.explanation: The inserted clause, "unless SMS offers a competing technology, in which case the option period is reduced to thirty [30] days", introduces an in-text contradiction. The paragraph initially states that GE and/or GM have sixty [60] days to exercise their option upon completion or termination of the Project. The added clause shortens this period to thirty [30] days if SMS offers a competing technology. This causes ambiguity, as GE and/or GM now face uncertainty about which deadline applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 Prototype Even Period. ---------------------- A. Options of GM and GE. Upon completion or other termination of the -------------------- Project, the parties agree that GE and/or GM shall have an option, exercisable upon written notice to SMS received within sixty [60] days after such completion or other termination, unless SMS offers a competing technology, in which case the option period is reduced to thirty [30] days, to have SMS maintain the "bread board" apparatus or the "demonstrator" apparatus from the Project and make such apparatus available to GE and/or GM for testing and/or evaluation purposes during a period of time ("Prototype Evaluation Period"). The Probe Evaluation Period shall begin upon receipt of such notice and end upon the earliest of: (1) the second anniversary of such completion or other termination, or (2) the delivery of at least one commercial Imaging System to each of GE and GM, or (3) the delivery of one commercial Imaging System to either GE or GM, where the other party fails to fulfill the requirements of paragraph 6.3 B2 below; or (4) SMS' delivery of the twelfth commercial Imaging System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by introducing a conditional timeline for exercising the option. Originally, GE and/or GM had sixty days to exercise their option after the completion or termination of the project. The added clause, 'unless SMS offers a competing technology, in which case the option period is reduced to thirty [30] days', creates a contradiction because it shortens the original sixty-day period under certain circumstances, creating ambiguity about the applicable deadline. The Contradicted Text, which is the same as the Changed Text, now contains conflicting information about the duration of the option period, which falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since it introduces conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 8.6 Publication. Except as set forth in Paragraph 8.7, each Participant ----------- reserves the right to copyright, publish, disclose, disseminate and use, in whole or in part, data and information generated or developed by such Participant in the performance of work in connection with the Project. At least sixty [60] days prior to the schedule date for publication or public disclosure, the publishing Participant shall provide a copy of the manuscript which is the subject of publication or presentation to the Management Committee for its review. Within such sixty [60] day period, the Management Committee shall review such manuscript and render its suggestions or comments, if any, to the publishing Participant. The publishing Participant shall not be bound to incorporate the Management Committee's suggestions and comments and, except as set forth in Paragraph 8.7 below, the publishing Participant's decision as to what the manuscript shall contain shall be final.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.6 Publication. Except as set forth in Paragraph 8.7, each Participant ----------- reserves the right to copyright, publish, disclose, disseminate and use, in whole or in part, data and information generated or developed by such Participant in the performance of work in connection with the Project. If the manuscript is of critical or sensitive nature, then the Project Manager must approve before public disclosure. At least sixty [60] days prior to the schedule date for publication or public disclosure, the publishing Participant shall provide a copy of the manuscript which is the subject of publication or presentation to the Management Committee for its review. Within such sixty [60] day period, the Management Committee shall review such manuscript and render its suggestions or comments, if any, to the publishing Participant. The publishing Participant shall not be bound to incorporate the Management Committee's suggestions and comments and, except as set forth in Paragraph 8.7 below, the publishing Participant's decision as to what the manuscript shall contain shall be final.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by requiring Project Manager approval for certain publications. The original text states that each Participant reserves the right to publish data and information, and that the publishing Participant is not bound to incorporate the Management Committee's suggestions, making the decision final. The inserted clause, "If the manuscript is of critical or sensitive nature, then the Project Manager must approve before public disclosure", contradicts this, as it now requires approval from the Project Manager, which undermines the publishing Participant's final decision-making authority. Now the approval and diclosure processes contradict one another.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.6 Publication. Except as set forth in Paragraph 8.7, each Participant ----------- reserves the right to copyright, publish, disclose, disseminate and use, in whole or in part, data and information generated or developed by such Participant in the performance of work in connection with the Project. If the manuscript is of critical or sensitive nature, then the Project Manager must approve before public disclosure. At least sixty [60] days prior to the schedule date for publication or public disclosure, the publishing Participant shall provide a copy of the manuscript which is the subject of publication or presentation to the Management Committee for its review. Within such sixty [60] day period, the Management Committee shall review such manuscript and render its suggestions or comments, if any, to the publishing Participant. The publishing Participant shall not be bound to incorporate the Management Committee's suggestions and comments and, except as set forth in Paragraph 8.7 below, the publishing Participant's decision as to what the manuscript shall contain shall be final.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that 'If the manuscript is of critical or sensitive nature, then the Project Manager must approve before public disclosure'. This directly contradicts the original statement and the reiterated text in the 'Contradicted Text' section, which states that each participant reserves the right to publish data and information, and the publishing participant's decision as to the manuscript's content shall be final, subject only to Paragraph 8.7. The added requirement of Project Manager approval creates a direct conflict with the participant's final decision-making authority, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where conflicting obligations are introduced.
